Step 1 of 4 — Google Sheet
Step 1 of 4
Create Your Google Sheet 📊
Click the green button below. Google Sheets opens.

You'll see a box asking you to name your copy — type your name and click the blue Make a copy button.

That's it! Come back here when done.
📊   Create My Personal Google Sheet
⚠️ Need a Google/Gmail account? Go to gmail.com first and create a free account. Takes 2 minutes.

What is Google Sheets?
Google Sheets is like Microsoft Excel but it lives on the internet and is completely free. Your retirement calculations — all 50 years of projections — live inside your Google Sheet. It does all the math automatically.
What is a Google account?
A Google account is a free account you create at gmail.com. If you already use Gmail for email, you already have one. Use the same login. If you don't have one, go to gmail.com, click "Create account" and follow the steps — it's free and takes about 2 minutes.
Why do I need to "Make a copy"?
The button opens a template — like a blank form. You need your own personal copy so your numbers are private and only visible to you. The original template stays untouched. Your copy is stored in your own Google Drive (your free Google storage space).
Step by step with pictures in your head:
1. Click the green button above — a new tab opens showing a spreadsheet
2. A box immediately appears asking what to name your copy — type your last name
3. Click the blue Make a copy button in that box
4. Your personal copy opens in a new tab — you can see your name in the title at the top
5. Come back to this tab and click Next
What if I don't see a box asking for a name?
You may need to sign into your Google account first. If Google asks you to sign in, enter your Gmail email address and password, then try the green button again.

Step 2 of 4
Add the Connection Script 🔧
This is the trickiest step — read carefully and follow one at a time.
In your Google Sheet:

1. Look at the menu INSIDE the spreadsheet (not your browser). Find Extensions and click it
2. Click Apps Script — a dark editor opens in a new tab
3. You see a file called Code.gs — DO NOT touch it
4. Click the + next to "Files" → click Script → type AppScript → press Enter
5. Click the button below to open the script file in a new tab
📄   Open Script File
6. In the script tab: press Ctrl+A (Windows) or Command+A (Mac) to select all
7. Press Ctrl+C or Command+C to copy
8. Go back to the Apps Script tab — click in the empty editor — press Ctrl+V or Command+V to paste
9. Press Ctrl+S or Command+S to save

Step 3 of 4
Turn It On & Get Your Link 🔗
Almost done! This creates the connection link between your sheet and dashboard.
In Apps Script:

1. Click the blue Deploy button (top right)
2. Click New deployment
3. Click the gear icon ⚙ → click Web app
4. Find Who has access → change it to Anyone
5. Click the blue Deploy button
⚠️ Google shows a scary warning — it's OK! Click Advanced → Go to AppScript (unsafe) → DO NOT click your email → click the blue Allow button
⚠️ DO NOT close the window yet! After clicking Allow, you'll see a long web address. Copy it and paste it in the box below — THEN you can close.
Starts with https://script.google.com/macros/s/ and ends with /exec
✅ Perfect! Click Next to generate your files.
⚠️ That doesn't look right — make sure you copied the whole address.
Closed the window already? Go to Apps Script → Deploy → Manage deployments → copy the URL from there.

What does "Deploy" mean?
Deploy just means "turn it on and make it available." When you deploy the script, Google creates a special web address — like a private door — that your dashboard uses to read your sheet. It sounds technical but you're just clicking a few buttons.
Where is the Deploy button?
In the Apps Script editor, look at the very top RIGHT corner of the page. You will see a blue button that says "Deploy". It's in the top right — not the middle, not the left. Click that blue button.
Why must "Who has access" say "Anyone"?
This setting controls who can use the connection. If it says "Only myself" your dashboard can't read your sheet — it only works when it says "Anyone." This doesn't mean your data is public — it means the connection itself is accessible. Your actual retirement numbers are always private in your Google Sheet.
The Google warning screen — exactly what to do:
1. You see: "Google hasn't verified this app" — this is completely normal for personal scripts
2. Click the small gray text at the bottom that says Advanced
3. Click the text that says Go to AppScript (unsafe) — yes, click it even though it says unsafe. It is safe.
4. A new screen appears showing your Google account with your name and email address
5. DO NOT click your email address or your name — those buttons do nothing useful
6. Look at the very bottom of the screen for a blue button that says Allow
7. Click Allow
Where is the URL after I click Allow?
After clicking Allow, Google shows a success screen. Look for a section that says Web app URL. Under those words is a long web address that starts with https://script.google.com/macros/s/ — that's your URL. There may be a Copy button next to it — click that. Or click the URL itself, press Command+A to select it all, then Command+C to copy it.
I already closed the window! How do I get my URL back?
Don't panic! Go back to the Apps Script tab in your browser. Click the blue Deploy button again. This time click Manage deployments instead of New deployment. Your deployment is listed there with the URL next to it. Copy it from there.

Step 4 of 4
Download Your Dashboard! 🎉
Personalizing your 7 dashboard pages with your Google Sheet connection...
Starting...
🎉
Your Dashboard Is Ready!

✅ After downloading:
1. Double-click the zip file to open it
2. Move all files into one folder — name it RetireBlueprint Pro
3. Double-click RetireBlueprintPro_Home.html to open in Chrome
